title: Monetary Contact Shocks
description: When previously separate civilizations begin trading, a local money can lose scarcity if outsiders replicate it at lower cost. This helps explain monetary inflation episodes tied to technology and trade contact.

resources.title: Aggry Beads and West African Currency Traditions
resources.notes: Historical anchor for bead-money and value shifts under foreign manufacture.

resources.title: Rai Stones (Wikipedia)
resources.notes: Parallel example of technology and transport changing monetary dynamics.

resources.title: The Ascent of Money
resources.notes: Comparative context for currency disruption events across regions.
